channel.title: Working from the Heart — Sunday Letters
channel.description: Weekly essays drawn from Friday support calls at Working from the Heart.

item.title: We Came Here Together
item.description: Why you trust your pets more than people, and what that says about the one relationship that's actually been reliable

item.title: Failure to Thrive
item.description: Her medical records said failure to thrive. The phrase had become a life pattern. What she discovered when she looked at what the pattern was for

item.title: Staying Small for You
item.description: She'd wanted it for years but kept it in the sky. What surfaced when she finally started moving toward it

item.title: I Just Want to Live
item.description: She'd been cycling in and out of the same pattern for years. What shifted when the frustration finally turned to boredom

item.title: A Standing Offer
item.description: The inner critic doesn't leave. What changed wasn't defeating it. It was finding a different way to stand in relation to it

item.title: Possessed by a Thought
item.description: Facing eviction and a hostile neighbor, something released. Not the situation, but the way she was holding it

item.title: No, But I Want There to Be
item.description: Is there a problem right now? No. But I want there to be. On the habit of manufacturing difficulty when things are actually fine

item.title: Love Wearing a Mask
item.description: She felt responsible for leaving her nephews behind. What the guilt revealed when she looked at it directly

item.title: The Anger Wants Me to Be Honest
item.description: He came in knowing he shouldn't be angry. When the anger finally spoke, it turned out to be telling the truth

item.title: The Bragging Right
item.description: She released the anxiety from an 8 to a 3. Something else stayed. On the record we carry alongside the weight itself

item.title: Did You Release, or Did Releasing Happen?
item.description: She hated her mind all week, then something shifted on its own. A question about agency, releasing, and who actually did it

item.title: The Mind Will Find One
item.description: When the crisis passes but the mind keeps scanning, why anxiety outlasts the problem that caused it

item.title: What If It Was Never the Enemy?
item.description: She'd been bracing since she was six. What shifted when she turned toward the fear instead of away from it

item.title: The Fear Beneath the Overwhelm
item.description: When a man came with a custody decision he couldn't make, releasing found what the decision was sitting on top of

item.title: The Middle Manager
item.description: Why the inner critic creates noise even when everything is fine, and what actually quiets it

item.title: Something in You Is Trying to Help
item.description: On procrastination, the nervous system, and what changes when you stop fighting what's underneath it

item.title: The Freedom to Say No
item.description: What happens when a facilitator says \u201cexcellent\u201d to someone who can't let go, and why the no is sometimes the release

item.title: Two Girls With Chalk
item.description: On staying present when the world is on fire

item.title: The Wednesday Question
item.description: Why I send a simple question to my community every Wednesday, and what it gives both of us

item.title: The Timestamps Were Wrong
item.description: I sent out wrong timestamps generated by AI. The mistake was small. What it revealed about presence and care in this work was not
